name: Chibcha

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Chibcha (chib1270).

notes: Chibchan language (Muisca) of the Colombian highlands, extinct since the late 18th century. There is no living pronunciation; phonemic values are RECONSTRUCTED from the colonial Spanish-based orthography of de Lugo (1619) and later grammars, following Constenla (1984) and Gonzalez de Perez (2006). Every value is tentative: c and qu both spell /k/, and the sibilant/affricate series z/ch/x (here read as retroflex) is the chief point of dispute. The grapheme y (an inverse-ypsilon in the sources) denotes the high central vowel. Stress, vowel length and nasalization are not recovered from the spelling.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Chibcha (chib1270)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
